medical_history.chief_complaint: Extreme mesomelic shortening of both lower limbs (with the fibulae more involved than the tibias), asymmetrical radio-ulnar shortening the left radius and ulna below the 5th centile in length and the right radius and ulna, both femurs and humeri of normal length for gestational age. Cutaneous syndactyly of the third and fourth fingers was noted bilaterally.
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Antenatal ultrasonographic findings revealed extreme mesomelic shortening of both lower limbs (with the fibulae more involved than the tibias), asymmetrical radio-ulnar shortening the left radius and ulna below the 5th centile in length and the right radius and ulna, both femurs and humeri of normal length for gestational age. Cutaneous syndactyly of the third and fourth fingers was noted bilaterally. A karyotype was normal (46,XX). A medical induction of labor took place at 20 weeks gestation.
medical_history.family_history: Parents were a non-consanguineous Chinese couple who had a healthy 2-year-old boy, were 33 and 38 years of age respectively.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Bilateral insertional polydactyly between the fourth and fifth digits of the hands in association with postaxial polydactyly resulting in seven fingers bilaterally, fibular hypoplasia, postaxial polysyndactyly (cutaneous and osseous) of both feet, an imperforate anus, a recto-vaginal fistula, vaginal atresia, and a hypothalamic hamartoma.

laboratory_examinations.findings: GLI3 c.3386_3387delTT mutation detected in exon 14

radiographic_examinations.findings: Extreme mesomelic shortening of both lower limbs (with the fibulae more involved than the tibias), asymmetrical radio-ulnar shortening the left radius and ulna below the 5th centile in length and the right radius and ulna, both femurs and humeri of normal length for gestational age. Cutaneous syndactyly of the third and fourth fingers was noted bilaterally.

Mesomelic changes in the ulna and tibia, fibular hypoplasia and blunted metaphyses, and decreased epiphyseal ossification. Mild radial bowing, post-axial polydactyly, and insertional polydactyly were evident.
Confirmed the presence of a hypothalamic tumor, bilateral hip and renal dysplasia, a laryngeal cleft, epiglottal hypoplasia, and a vascular ring.

other_tests.findings: Bilateral insertional polydactyly between the fourth and fifth digits of the hands in association with postaxial polydactyly resulting in seven fingers bilaterally, fibular hypoplasia, postaxial polysyndactyly (cutaneous and osseous) of both feet, an imperforate anus, a recto-vaginal fistula, vaginal atresia, and a hypothalamic hamartoma.

Bilateral lower limb acromesomelia, bilateral brachydactyly, left fourth and fifth finger cutaneous syndactyly, insertional left third toe polydactyly, digital nail hypoplasia, left fifth and sixth toe cutaneous syndactyly, right fourth and fifth toe cutaneous syndactyly, and an anteriorly placed anus with stenosis.
